title: ACME Hedge Finder Dashboard
subtitle: Synthetic strict-mode example for public-equity hedge selection.

metadata.source_posture: Synthetic thesis, portfolio, and hedge-candidate examples
metadata.decision_context: Whether the unwanted market and growth-factor exposure should be hedged or managed by a smaller equity size.

hero.headline: Hedge beta without hedging away the stock thesis
hero.dek: The dashboard separates retained exposure, basis risk, and readiness gaps.
hero.callout: Use a liquid equity-index hedge only if it preserves the idiosyncratic product thesis; route credit hedges to Credit Markets.

snapshot.label: Recommendation
snapshot.detail: Preserves product-specific upside while reducing broad market drawdown risk.

snapshot.label: Readiness
snapshot.detail: Live pricing, risk model, and option-chain inputs must be refreshed before implementation.

data.label: Hedge read
data.summary: Prefer a partial index or sector hedge over a credit instrument; size down if basis risk is worse than the exposure.
data.stock_skew: Retained exposure is the product catalyst and estimate-revision path.

rows.Mechanism: Reduces broad market beta while retaining issuer-specific thesis.
Targets sector and factor exposure more directly.
rows.Basis risk: Can introduce independent short squeeze risk.

rows.Mechanism: Credit-risk signal only in this plugin.
rows.Basis risk: Credit hedge construction routes to Credit Markets.

cases.label: Thesis right
cases.summary: Equity alpha remains if the hedge does not remove product-specific upside.

cases.label: Hedge fails
cases.summary: Proxy breaks if software factor sells off more than the broad index.

events.impact: Determines whether to resize, roll, remove, or size down instead.

items: Live pricing, correlations, borrow, option-chain depth, and risk-model exposure are required before calling the hedge implementation-ready.
Credit hedge candidates require a Credit Markets handoff rather than local implementation.

sources.title: Synthetic hedge-candidate snapshot
sources.excerpt: Candidate hedge, readiness, and basis-risk placeholders.
